title: paeonia obovata subsp. japonica
intro: Paeonia obovata subsp. japonica, also known as Japanese peony, is a subspecies of flowering plant native to East Asia, characterized by its perennial growth habit and showy flowers. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Paeonia obovata subsp. japonica include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, improve sleep quality, and support immune function, among others. In traditional herbal medicine, Paeonia obovata subsp. japonica is often prepared as a root extract or dried root, which can be used in tea, tincture, or capsule form.
